filemgr.ws.external_desc: Create a one-time invite for a new external DNA Connect identity. After they accept, give them the member access link for future visits.

filemgr.ws.external_send_hint: Send the one-time invite link to the outsider. They open it and scan the QR with DNA Connect. After they accept, send them the member access link for future visits.

settings.page.title: Settings
settings.page.subtitle: Personal DNA-Nexus preferences
settings.general.title: General settings
settings.general.desc: These settings affect your own browser. They do not change the global admin theme.
settings.profile.title: Profile
settings.profile.desc: Edit your personal profile. Role, status, quota, storage, and admin notes are not editable here.
settings.profile.name: Name
settings.profile.avatar_help: You can pick a normal photo. DNA-Nexus will resize it to a small avatar automatically. PNG, JPEG, and WebP work best.
settings.theme.title: Theme
settings.theme.desc: Choose how DNA-Nexus looks on this device.
settings.theme.dark.title: DNA Dark
settings.theme.bright.title: Bright
settings.theme.cpunk_orange.title: CPUNK Orange
settings.theme.win_classic.title: Win Classic
people.title: People
people.subtitle: Friendly names for DNA fingerprints and workspace collaborators
people.hero_desc: Save friendly names for DNA fingerprints, local users, external DNA Connect members, and workspace collaborators. These names are private to you and do not rename anyone globally.
people.module_missing: The People UI module did not load. Hard-refresh the browser and try again.
people.no_saved_desc: Add your first fingerprint label. Later, workspace members and external invite flows can offer “Add to People” automatically.
people.future_desc: These labels will later appear in @mentions, file locks, comments, activity timelines, “what changed?” digests, and access maps.
people.notes_placeholder: Private note, e.g. John from motorbike club
people.editor.private_label_desc: This only changes your private label for this fingerprint. It does not rename the real user globally.
people.editor.detached_desc: Private label for fingerprints, local users, and external DNA Connect identities. This does not rename anyone globally.
people.confirm.title: Confirm action
people.remove.title: Remove from People?
people.remove.subtitle: This removes your private label for this person.
people.remove.note: This does not delete the real user, external member, or any files. It only removes the saved People label.
admin.settings.title: Admin Settings
admin.settings.notes.title: Notes
admin.settings.notes.body: Settings are admin-only. They affect logging going forward. Already-written audit lines remain unchanged.
admin.settings.top.title: Admin • Settings
admin.audit.verbosity_note: Meaning: DEBUG logs everything • INFO hides noisy lifecycle • ADMIN is default • SECURITY keeps only security-relevant events. Note: already-written audit lines remain; this only affects what gets appended going forward.
admin.audit.rotation_note: Rotation renames the active pqnas_audit.jsonl into a timestamped archive and starts a fresh active log. Retention rules apply only to rotated archives.
admin.audit.rot.size_mb: When active log exceeds N MB
admin.audit.rotation_policy_note: Automatic rotation is enforced server-side (best-effort) before appending audit lines. Retention rules still apply only to rotated archives, never the active log.
admin.audit.ret.size_mb: Keep up to N MB total (rotated files)
admin.audit.retention_safety: Safety: retention prunes rotated archive files only. The active audit log (pqnas_audit.jsonl) is never deleted by retention.
admin.audit.preview_note: Preview is a dry-run: it lists what would be deleted. “Run prune now” deletes only those candidates, and logs an admin audit event.
admin.snap.title: Snapshots (Btrfs)
admin.snap.note1: If Automatic schedule is unchecked while snapshots remain enabled, snapshots are manual only: users/admins can still trigger “snapshot now”, but the scheduler does not run. If per-volume schedule is enabled, each volume can override the global times/day + jitter. Otherwise the global schedule applies to all volumes.
admin.snap.note2: Uses btrfs subvolume snapshot -r. The destination must be an empty path (server cleans up if it exists). If snapshots are disabled, settings stay visible but inputs are greyed out.
admin.uploads.title: Uploads
admin.uploads.note1: These values control how often the migration worker runs and how aggressively it drains landing storage.
admin.uploads.note2: Upload tiering lets new uploads land in a fast pool first, then migrate to capacity storage. Select an internal pool for landing. USB / removable pools are automatically blocked for safety. The migration worker later moves files to capacity pools according to tiering policy.
admin.uploads.help_body:
Upload limits

Upload tiering
New uploads land in a fast internal pool first and later migrate to capacity storage.

USB / removable pools are automatically blocked for safety.

Worker interval
How often the migration worker wakes up and scans landing storage.

Minimum landing age
A file must remain in landing at least this long before migration is allowed.

Max files per pass
Maximum number of eligible files migrated during one worker scan.

Example:
interval = 60
min age = 60
max/pass = 8

Worker wakes every 60 seconds, migrates files older than 60 seconds, and processes up to 8 files each cycle.
admin.dna.title: DNA Connect alerts
admin.dna.note1: PQ-NAS can send warnings to DNA Connect by invoking dna-connect-cli.
admin.dna.note2: A dedicated PQ-NAS DNA identity must already exist in the configured DNA data directory, and the recipient should already have accepted that identity as a contact.
admin.dna.note3: The test button sends a real message using the current saved settings. This is optional and fully admin-controlled.
admin.theme.title: UI Theme
admin.theme.desc: Theme is saved in admin_settings.json and also cached in your browser so all pages can apply it instantly.
admin.confirm.title: Confirm action
admin.confirm.run_prune_sub: This deletes rotated audit archives according to the saved retention policy.
admin.confirm.rotate_sub: This closes the current audit log and starts a fresh active log.
admin.stats.top_sub: Storage overview, file counts, MIME groups, users, and workspaces
admin.users.top_sub: Profile, role, and storage metadata • saved to users.json
admin.users.avatar_placeholder: avatar (click to choose; image will be resized…)
admin.users.help: Use Enable to allow logins, Disable to require approval again, and Revoke to hard-block that fingerprint. Delete removes the entry entirely (cleanup).
admin.users.load_edit_title: Load this user into the edit form
admin.users.enable_title: Allow this fingerprint to log in again
admin.users.disable_title: Disable login until an admin enables it again
admin.users.revoke_title: Hard-block this fingerprint from logging in
admin.users.allocate_title: Allocate storage and set quota for this user
admin.users.migrate_title: Move user storage to another pool with async copy and verify
admin.users.delete_title: Remove this entry from users.json; the user can reappear if they scan again
admin.users.self_protection: Self-protection: enable / disable / revoke / delete are blocked for your own fingerprint.
admin.users.refuse_self: Refusing to modify your own admin entry (prevents lockout or role change).
admin.users.amount_help: Set a positive quota to allow uploads.
admin.users.warn_below_used: Requested quota is below current used space.
admin.users.warn_over_alloc: Requested quota exceeds remaining allocatable capacity on this pool.
admin.users.migrate_help: Migration copies user data to the destination pool, verifies it, then switches the user’s storage mapping. Source data is kept for safety.
admin.users.delete_confirm_sub: This removes the entry from users.json.
admin.users.delete_confirm_note: This removes the entry entirely as cleanup. If they scan again, they will re-appear as disabled.
admin.users.revoke_confirm_sub: This hard-blocks login for this fingerprint.
admin.users.revoke_confirm_note: Use this when this identity should not be allowed to log in again.
admin.users.avatar_read_failed: Could not read this image. Try PNG, JPEG, or WebP.
admin.users.avatar_too_large: Avatar is still too large after resizing ({size}). Try a smaller image.
admin.users.avatar_uploaded_toast:
Avatar uploaded
{note}Click Upsert to save this avatar URL into the user profile.
admin.users.already_allocated_confirm:
Storage is already allocated for this user.

Change pool/quota anyway?
admin.users.storage_required_migration: Storage must be allocated before migration.
admin.users.destination_diff: Destination pool must differ from current pool.
admin.users.migrate_confirm:
Migrate user storage to pool "{pool}"?

This will create an async job. The worker will copy data, verify it, then switch the user's storage mapping.
admin.users.migration_job_missing: Migration job_id missing from server response
admin.users.migration_timeout_detail: Migration polling timed out. Job is still on server; reopen status later.
admin.users.cleanup_sub: Choose the old pool copy to remove for this user.
admin.users.old_pool_placeholder: old pool id, for example raidtest
admin.users.cleanup_note: Only the old inactive storage copy should be removed. The active pool is protected.
admin.users.old_pool_active_error: Old pool id cannot be the active pool.
admin.users.pool_id_not_path: Use a pool id, not a path.
admin.users.cleanup_prompt:
Cleanup old copy from which pool?

User is currently active on {pool}.
Enter old pool id to delete, or use "default" if the stale copy is there.
admin.users.old_pool_must_differ: Old pool must differ from the active pool.
admin.users.cleanup_job_missing: Cleanup job_id missing from server response
admin.users.cleanup_not_needed_toast:
Old storage cleanup not needed
No old inactive copy was found.
admin.approvals.top_sub: Enable disabled users after they scan • saved to users.json
admin.approvals.title: Approvals
admin.approvals.help: This view focuses on identities needing action (disabled / revoked). Use Enable to approve login. Delete removes the entry entirely (cleanup).
admin.approvals.delete_sub: This removes the user from users.json.
admin.approvals.delete_note: This removes the entry entirely as cleanup. If they scan again, they will re-appear as disabled.
admin.approvals.revoke_sub: This hard-blocks login for this fingerprint.
admin.approvals.revoke_note: Use this when the identity should not be allowed to log in again.
admin.apps.sidebar_note: Upload app zip → validate → install. Manage installed apps.
admin.apps.top_sub: Upload app zip → validate → install. Manage installed apps.
admin.apps.install_help: Zip must contain manifest.json and www/index.html.
admin.apps.admin_only_install: Admin only — hide from normal users
admin.apps.uninstall_sub: This removes the installed app package from this server.
admin.apps.uninstall_note: This removes the installed app files and registration for this version. User data stored elsewhere is not intentionally deleted.
admin.audit_ui.sidebar_note: This page only renders and verifies. It never modifies audit log contents.
admin.audit_ui.top_sub: Hash-chained JSONL security audit trail (tail + integrity verification)
admin.audit_ui.tip: Tip: click a row to expand full JSON. Use Esc to collapse all.
admin.audit_ui.interval_title: Auto-refresh interval in seconds (used when Auto is enabled)
admin.audit_ui.footer_note: Expand rows for raw JSON (monospace). This page does not modify the audit log and does not attempt to “fix” anything — it only renders and verifies.
admin.audit_ui.rotate_sub: This creates a rotated archive and starts a fresh active audit log.
admin.audit_ui.rotate_action: Create rotated file and write rotate_header
admin.audit_ui.rotate_note: The page refreshes the tail and verifies integrity after rotation.
admin.overview.sidebar_hint: Manage users, review audit logs, and verify server integrity.
system.title: System
system.cpu_note: Load is system-wide. Usage is sampled from /proc/stat deltas.
system.drive_health_note: Health data is shown for internal storage devices that expose SMART/NVMe diagnostics.
system.process_note: This is the DNA-Nexus server process. No admin rights needed to view.
system.selftest_unavailable: Self-test start not available for this drive.
system.confirm_extended_selftest: Start extended self-test for {dev}? This may take a long time.
